b (beta) decay
g (gamma) rays

autoradiography

-log [H+] = pH    definition of pH
-log Ka = pKa    definition of pKa
pKa = pH  +  log

pKa = pH  +  log
when [X]  =  [HX]        log            =  log 1 = 0
pKa = pH  +  0
pKa = pH at which [X] and [HX] are equal
XH                X  +  H+

pKa = 6.3
at pH = 4.0  (add acid = H+ donor)
XH           X-  +  H+  +  H+
~50%            ~50%
~100%                 0
at pH = 8.0  (add base B, picks up H+)
XH           X-  +  H+
0                 ~100%
BH+
pH < pKa
pH > pKa

The words “protonated” and “deprotonated” tell you
NOTHING about the net charge on a molecule.
When a molecule becomes protonated, it gains a proton
(H+) and the charge increases by 1
When a molecule becomes deprotonated, it loses a proton
and the charge decreases by 1 relative to the protonated form
NH4             NH3  +  H+
+
H2O              OH-  +  H+
HSO4                   SO4  +  H+
